product_hunt.tagline: 5 personal finance tools for the price of one month of budgeting apps
product_hunt.description:
FinanceKit is a personal finance toolkit built with Python and Streamlit. It includes 5 modules:

📄 Receipt Scanner — Snap a photo of any receipt and automatically extract merchant, amount, date, and category using OCR.

📈 Portfolio Tracker — Track your investments without connecting to your brokerage. Privacy-first, no data sharing.

📊 Report Generator — Create clean, professional financial reports you can share with your accountant or use for your own records.

💼 Job Tracker — Built for freelancers and gig workers. Track jobs, invoices, and payments in one place.

🔍 Subscription Auditor — See every recurring charge in one view. Find the subscriptions you forgot about (we all have them).

Why FinanceKit?
• $29.99 one-time payment (no subscription)
• Runs locally — your financial data stays on your machine
• Built with Python — customize and extend it if you want
• Free live demo available

Perfect for freelancers, gig workers, college students, and anyone who wants real finance tools without the monthly fee.
product_hunt.first_comment:
Hey Product Hunt! 👋

I'm the maker of FinanceKit. I built this because I was frustrated with the state of personal finance tools — they're either too expensive (QuickBooks), too limited (basic budget trackers), or they got shut down (RIP Mint).

I wanted something that:
1. Doesn't cost me $15/month forever
2. Keeps my financial data local and private
3. Actually covers the tools I use daily

So I built it with Python and Streamlit. The demo is fully functional — try it at https://financekit.streamlit.app before buying.

I'd love your feedback. What finance tools are you currently using? What's missing from them?

And if you find a bug in the demo, let me know — I'll fix it today. 😄
product_hunt.maker_comment:
Thanks for checking out FinanceKit! A few things I wanted to highlight:

• The demo at https://financekit.streamlit.app is fully functional, not a mockup
• The receipt scanner uses OCR and is about 90% accurate in my testing
• Everything runs locally — I never see your financial data
• The source code for the demo is on GitHub: https://github.com/brandocalricia/financekit-demo

I'm actively developing this and have a roadmap based on user feedback. Bank statement import and budget forecasting are coming next.

Happy to answer any questions!

hacker_news.title: Show HN: FinanceKit – Personal finance toolkit built with Python and Streamlit
hacker_news.body:
I built a personal finance toolkit with 5 modules: receipt scanning (OCR), portfolio tracking, report generation, job/gig tracking, and subscription auditing.

Tech: Python 3.11, Streamlit, Tesseract OCR for receipts, Pandas for data processing.

Live demo: https://financekit.streamlit.app
Source (demo version): https://github.com/brandocalricia/financekit-demo
Full version ($29.99): https://5207453582610.gumroad.com/l/zbnsjc

The receipt scanner was the hardest part — getting reliable OCR on crumpled receipts with bad lighting is a non-trivial problem. I ended up preprocessing images (deskew, contrast enhancement, noise reduction) before running OCR which improved accuracy from ~60% to ~90%.

Built this because I freelance and was paying for QuickBooks + YNAB + a spreadsheet to cover what this one tool does. Figured others might find it useful too.

Feedback welcome, especially on the demo.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: I sell a Python/Streamlit app on Gumroad for $30 – here's how it works
hacker_news.body:
Interesting experiment in selling a Python tool as a product. FinanceKit is a personal finance toolkit (receipt scanner, portfolio tracker, report generator, job tracker, subscription auditor) built with Streamlit.

I sell the full version on Gumroad for $29.99 (one-time) and host a free demo on Streamlit Community Cloud.

Source for the demo: https://github.com/brandocalricia/financekit-demo
Live demo: https://financekit.streamlit.app

The distribution model is interesting — Python apps aren't traditionally sold as products. I went with Gumroad because:
1. No need to build payment infrastructure
2. Handles license keys
3. 10% cut is reasonable

The free demo on Streamlit Cloud acts as the primary conversion driver. People try it, see it works, and buy.

Curious if others here have experience selling Python tools as products. What worked for you?

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Open-source personal finance demo with receipt OCR
hacker_news.body:
Open source demo of FinanceKit, a personal finance toolkit I'm building.

GitHub: https://github.com/brandocalricia/financekit-demo
Live: https://financekit.streamlit.app

The demo includes all 5 modules with limited functionality. The receipt scanner is the most interesting technically — it uses OCR with image preprocessing (deskew, contrast normalization, noise reduction) to extract structured data from receipt photos.

Stack: Python, Streamlit, Tesseract, Pandas.

Looking for contributors and feedback on the OCR pipeline specifically. PRs welcome.

linkedin.text:
After years of juggling QuickBooks, spreadsheets, and budgeting apps, I decided to build the finance tool I actually wanted.

The result is FinanceKit — a personal finance toolkit with 5 modules:

• Receipt Scanner (OCR-powered)
• Portfolio Tracker
• Report Generator
• Job Tracker (built for freelancers)
• Subscription Auditor

What makes it different:
→ $29.99 one-time. No monthly subscription.
→ Runs locally. Your financial data stays on your machine.
→ Built with Python, so you can customize it.

I built this because I'm a freelancer and I was spending more time managing my finance tools than actually managing my finances.

If you're a freelancer, gig worker, or small business owner who's tired of overpaying for financial software — check out the free demo:

https://financekit.streamlit.app

Full toolkit: https://5207453582610.gumroad.com/l/zbnsjc

I'd love to hear what financial tools you're currently using and what's missing from them.

I ran the Subscription Auditor from my finance toolkit on my own accounts last month.

Found $47/month in subscriptions I'd forgotten about:
• Cloud storage I migrated away from 6 months ago
• A meal kit service that "unpaused" itself
• A streaming trial that auto-converted
• A domain I no longer use

That's $564/year.

This is exactly why I built FinanceKit. It's easy to lose track of recurring charges when they're spread across multiple cards and accounts.

The Subscription Auditor module gives you one clear view of every recurring charge. No connecting bank accounts required — it works with the data you provide.

Free demo: https://financekit.streamlit.app

How much do you think you're spending on forgotten subscriptions?

Tax season reminder for freelancers:

If you're not tracking receipts throughout the year, April is going to hurt.

I built a Receipt Scanner module into FinanceKit that lets you:
→ Snap a photo of any receipt
→ Automatically extract merchant, amount, date, and line items
→ Categorize expenses
→ Generate reports your accountant will actually thank you for

It uses OCR and runs locally — your receipt data never leaves your machine.

The full toolkit (5 modules including portfolio tracking, subscription auditing, and job tracking) is $29.99 one-time on Gumroad.

Free demo: https://financekit.streamlit.app

What's your current system for tracking business expenses?

The economics of personal finance software don't make sense to me.

YNAB: $14.99/month
QuickBooks Self-Employed: $15/month
Mint: shut down entirely

You're paying $180+/year for basic financial tracking. And if the company decides to shut down (looking at you, Mint), you lose everything.

That's why I built FinanceKit:
• 5 finance modules (receipt scanner, portfolio tracker, reports, job tracker, subscription auditor)
• $29.99 one-time payment
• Runs locally on your machine
• Built with Python — you own the code

It's not as polished as a VC-backed SaaS product. But it works, it's affordable, and it won't disappear when a company pivots.

Try the demo: https://financekit.streamlit.app

Full version: https://5207453582610.gumroad.com/l/zbnsjc

Lessons from launching my first digital product:

1. A working demo converts better than any landing page. I put FinanceKit's demo live at https://financekit.streamlit.app and it outperformed every other marketing effort combined.

2. Price anchoring matters. At $29.99 one-time vs. $15/month for competitors, the value proposition is immediately obvious.

3. Solve your own problem first. I built FinanceKit because I needed it as a freelancer. Turns out other freelancers needed it too.

4. Don't wait for perfection. I launched with "good enough" and improved based on actual user feedback.

5. Gumroad makes distribution easy. 10% cut, but zero infrastructure headaches.

FinanceKit is a personal finance toolkit with 5 modules: receipt scanning, portfolio tracking, report generation, job tracking, and subscription auditing.

If you're thinking about launching a digital product — just do it. The worst that happens is you learn a lot.

Small business owners: how much time do you spend on financial admin each week?

I used to spend 3-4 hours every week on:
• Entering receipts into spreadsheets
• Checking which subscriptions hit this month
• Updating my investment tracker
• Formatting reports for my accountant

Now I spend about 30 minutes using FinanceKit.

The receipt scanner alone saves me an hour a week. Instead of typing in receipt data, I scan it and the OCR handles extraction.

I built this tool for myself but it turns out a lot of people have the same problem.

Free demo: https://financekit.streamlit.app

$29.99 one-time for the full version. No subscriptions, no recurring fees.

Time is money — especially when you're running a small business.
